title: Arirang World Tour Goyang Night 3 — 'Come Over' Debuts Live; Three-Night Korea Run Closes
detail: BTS closes the three-night Goyang Stadium opening run on April 12, 2026 with a show featuring the live debut of 'Come Over' — the vinyl-exclusive hidden track produced by Suga. The final Goyang performance draws critical praise for its traditional Korean production: gayageum melodies, ink-wash painting visuals, choreography inspired by Seungmu and Ganggangsullae, and stage motifs drawn from Korea's national flag and Gyeonghoeru pavilion. Jungkook addressed the crowd on improved weather compared to the rain-soaked opening night. The Korea Times review noted the IDOL stadium circuit as 'a victory lap that felt almost like a statement of K-pop's global reach.' The full 34-song setlist opened with 'Hooligans' and 'Aliens' and closed with encores 'Butter,' 'Dynamite,' 'Please,' and 'Into the Sun.' Fans gathered outside the stadium in the thousands to listen to the show, unable to obtain tickets. With the three Goyang dates complete, the ARIRANG World Tour moves to Tokyo Dome.

media.caption: BTS closes the three-night Goyang run with a fiery finale featuring the live debut of 'Come Over'

title: ARIRANG Tracking Toward Unprecedented Third Consecutive Billboard 200 No. 1
detail: As of April 12, 2026, UPI and music industry analysts report ARIRANG is on track for a third consecutive week at #1 on the Billboard 200 (April 18-dated chart) — which would be the longest consecutive run at the top by a K-pop album in the chart's history. The album continues to dominate streaming and sales in its third week of release, buoyed by the opening of the ARIRANG World Tour and the Goyang concerts drawing massive global media attention. A third consecutive #1 would extend BTS's record and surpass any prior K-pop act's chart peak.

media.caption: ARIRANG tracking toward a third consecutive Billboard 200 No. 1 — unprecedented for K-pop
